purpose.summary: The DBUNLDGS program extracts authorization summary and detail data from an IMS database and inserts it into a GSAM database. It reads pending authorization summaries and details, then writes them to a GSAM dataset for reporting or archival purposes.
purpose.business_context: This program likely supports auditing or reporting on authorization activities within a larger system.

inputs.name: PAUTBPCB
inputs.description: IMS database containing pending authorization summary and detail segments.

inputs.name: PASFLPCB
inputs.description: IMS database used for GSAM output.

outputs.name: GSAM Database
outputs.description: GSAM database containing extracted authorization summary and detail records.

called_programs.purpose: Performs IMS database calls to retrieve and insert segments.

Performs IMS database calls to retrieve and insert segments.

business_rules.description: Only numeric PA-ACCT-ID values are processed.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks if PA-ACCT-ID is numeric before writing the record.

business_rules.description: The program stops processing authorization summaries when the IMS database returns a 'GB' status code.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS for 'GB' to determine the end of the authorization database.

business_rules.description: The program stops processing authorization details when the IMS database returns a 'GE' status code.
business_rules.logic_summary: The program checks the PAUT-PCB-STATUS for 'GE' to determine the end of the authorization details.

transforms.transformation_description: Moves the account ID to the root segment key for GSAM insertion.

paragraphs.purpose: This is the program ID paragraph. It does not contain any executable code and serves only to identify the program.

This paragraph serves as the main control loop for the DBUNLDGS program. It first calls 1000-INITIALIZE to perform necessary setup tasks. Then, it repeatedly calls 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY until WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG is set to 'Y', indicating the end of the root segment data. Finally, it calls 4000-FILE-CLOSE to close the output file and terminate the program. The paragraph orchestrates the overall process of extracting authorization data from an IMS database and writing it to a GSAM file.

This paragraph initializes the program by accepting the current date and displaying startup messages. It accepts the current date from the DATE special register and the current Julian date from the DAY special register. It then displays a series of messages to the console, including the program name and the current date. This paragraph prepares the program for processing by setting up initial values and providing informational output.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 1000-INITIALIZE paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization summary segment from the IMS database. It initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS and then calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GN to retrieve the next segment into PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY using ROOT-UNQUAL-SSA. If the PAUT-PCB-STATUS is spaces, it increments counters, moves the data to OPFIL1-REC, initializes ROOT-SEG-KEY and CHILD-SEG-REC, moves PA-ACCT-ID to ROOT-SEG-KEY, and then calls 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM to write the parent segment to GSAM. After writing the parent, it calls 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL to process the child segments until WS-END-OF-CHILD-SEG is 'Y'. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is 'GB', it sets END-OF-AUTHDB to TRUE and WS-END-OF-ROOT-SEG to 'Y'. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is neither spaces nor 'GB', it displays error messages and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 2000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-SUMMARY paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph retrieves the next authorization detail segment from the IMS database. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-GNP to retrieve the next segment into PENDING-AUTH-DETAILS using CHILD-UNQUAL-SSA. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is spaces, it sets MORE-AUTHS to TRUE, increments counters, moves the data to CHILD-SEG-REC, and calls 3200-INSERT-CHILD-SEG-GSAM to write the child segment to GSAM. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is 'GE', it moves 'Y' to WS-END-OF-CHILD-SEG. If PAUT-PCB-STATUS is neither spaces nor 'GE', it displays error messages and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. Finally, it initializes PAUT-PCB-STATUS.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 3000-FIND-NEXT-AUTH-DTL paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

This paragraph inserts the parent segment into the GSAM database. It calls CBLTDLI with FUNC-ISRT using PASFLPCB and PENDING-AUTH-SUMMARY. If PASFL-PCB-STATUS is not spaces, it displays error messages and calls 9999-ABEND to terminate the program. This paragraph handles the insertion of the parent authorization summary segment into the GSAM output.
This paragraph is a standard exit point for the 3100-INSERT-PARENT-SEG-GSAM paragraph. It contains only the EXIT statement, which returns control to the calling paragraph.

error_handling.condition: PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GB'
PAUT-PCB-STATUS NOT EQUAL TO SPACES AND 'GE'

open_questions.question: What is the structure of the IMS database segments (PAUTBPCB, PASFLPCB, PADFLPCB)?
open_questions.context: The copybooks for these segments are not provided, making it difficult to fully understand the data being processed.
open_questions.suggestion: Obtain the copybooks for these segments to understand the data layout and fields.

open_questions.question: What is the format and purpose of the GSAM output file?
open_questions.context: The program writes data to a GSAM file, but the structure and intended use of this file are unclear.
open_questions.suggestion: Investigate the GSAM file definition and its consumers to understand its purpose.
